page: Microsoft Azure DS13 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DS family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 8 vCPUs. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

The instance is configured with 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core. It includes 115 GB of local storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. In terms of performance, the DS13 exhibits a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, consistently fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core score, and Passmark CPU score, perform within the average middle 50% range.

Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with Passmark memory scores, place the instance in the average tier. Database operations, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations also align with average performance expectations. For machine learning workloads, the instance handles small-model LLM inference and medium-model text generation at average speeds, though medium-model prompt processing drops to the bottom 25% tier. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and average multi-threaded performance, the DS13 provides a balanced resource density for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and general-purpose applications that do not require high single-threaded CPU throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure DS13 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores (8 vCPUs) operating at 2.6 GHz, paired with 56.0 GB of memory and 115 GB of local storage. Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, rank in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks consistently achieve average, middle-50% results. This resource configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from larger memory footprints rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed. The instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring moderate multi-threaded compute capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS13 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, 56.0 GB of memory, and 115 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS13 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 56.0 GB of RAM, and 115 GB of local storage for balanced workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering eight dedicated cores, a high memory-to-core ratio, and integrated local storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.

56.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
Average multi-core compute and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
